title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Program Brief: Reader Outcome
summary: A team says “build a blog generator” but has not defined readers, publishing authority, content ownership, supported routes, accessibility needs, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reader outcome increment in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Reader Outcome, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_outcomes_repo_architecture_4(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Reader Outcome evidence · Theory Reader Outcome
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-reader-outcome-j3cvpt" that uses case 7979 to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-reader-outcome-j3cvpt
# Competency: ssg-reader-outcome.
# Case 7979: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_reader_outcome_j3cvpt(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_j3cvpt = "j3cvpt-tpvc3j-7979"
    assert evidence_variant_j3cvpt
    evidence_axes_j3cvpt = (
        "readerj3cvpt",
        "source7979",
        "parsetpvc3j",
        "routej3cvpt7979",
        "artifact7979j3cvpt",
        "recoverytpvc3j7979",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_j3cvpt)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Reader Outcome model · Theory Reader Outcome
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Reader Outcome: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9577 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Reader Outcome model · Theory Reader Outcome.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Reader Outcome model · Theory Reader Outcome, keep Reader Outcome connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
